assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics created with by-repository grouping
Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>'
Each task specifies its parent Epic key — backend tasks to backend Epic, frontend tasks to frontend Epic
The plan documents Incorporates links from Feature to each Epic
Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics
Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections
The plan documents Epic creation with level-1 issue type and parent set to feature issue key
Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name
Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections
For each task created, a description digest is produced with format-tagged SHA-256 hash

assertion_results.evidence: No convention enrichment sections with prescribed 'Applies:' rationale format found in any task description file.

assertion_results.text: Priority is inherited (Major) when set on Feature
fixVersions are inherited when set on Feature
A summary of inherited field propagation is produced
